content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat   Personal   Insurance   Complimentary mobile phone insurance   Complimentary mobile phone insurance   Enjoy complimentary mobile phone insurance against accidental damage and theft with a NAB Rewards Signature Card.  *   View Disclaimer 1  Summary of cover   This complimentary mobile phone insurance covers you for accidental damage and theft up to $1,650 p.a.   Your eligible mobile phone will be covered from the first day of the following month after you have paid your monthly mobile plan bill with your NAB Rewards Signature Card.    Your cover will continue as long as you keep paying your monthly mobile phone bills with your NAB Rewards Signature Card. If you miss one payment, your cover will be suspended for the following month.  Eligibility   To be eligible, customers must:  have a NAB Rewards Signature Card  have a contract with a mobile phone provider  be on a monthly billing arrangement  pay their monthly phone plan each month with their NAB Rewards Signature Card.  What will mobile phone insurance cover?  Accidental Damage – your eligible phone can no longer perform the function they were intended for due to broken parts or material or structural failures due to an accident.  Theft.  Insurance coverage  $50 excess per claim  We will pay up to $1,000 per claim (with an annual limit of $1,650).  Exclusions  Exclusions include:  Mobile telephone accessories other than the standard battery and/or standard antenna provided by the manufacturer.  Eligible Mobile Telephones that are lost or Mysteriously Disappear, as defined in the terms and conditions.  Eligible Mobile Telephones under the care and control of a common carrier, including, but not limited to, the postal service, airlines or delivery service, and Eligible Mobile Telephone is not under the Eligible Cardholder`s possession at time of the incident.  Eligible Mobile Telephones stolen from baggage whilst travelling unless hand-carried and under the Eligible Cardholder`s supervision or under the supervision of the Eligible Cardholder`s traveling companion who is previously known to the Eligible Cardholder.  Cosmetic damage such as a dent or scratch to the Eligible Mobile Telephone or Accidental Damage that does not impact the Eligible Mobile Telephone`s operation and functions (including minor screen cracks and fractures less than 5 cm in length that does not prevent the ability to make or receive phone calls or to use other operations and functions of the Eligible Mobile Telephone).  Accidental Damage or Theft resulting from any intentional acts or omission by You or anyone acting on Your behalf, willful damage, destruction or misplacement of the Eligible Mobile Telephone, fraud, hostilities of any kind (including, but not limited to, War, invasion, rebellion or insurrection), confiscation by the authorities, risks of contraband, illegal activities, normal wear and tear, flood, earthquake, radioactive contamination, or damage from inherent product defects or vermin.  Accidental Damage or Theft resulting from mis-delivery or non-return of the Eligible Mobile Telephone lent or voluntarily given to a third party.  A mobile telephone purchased for resale or commercial use. Subject to the terms of the terms and conditions, Insurance Coverage is for personal mobile phone devices and for use by Eligible Cardholder for personal purposes only.  Electrical, electronic or mechanical defects where there is no evidence of Accidental Damage.  How to file a claim  The Eligible Cardholder must file a claim as soon as reasonably practicable, and provide:  Completed and signed claim form as soon as reasonably practicable.  Eligible Cardholder’s card statement reflecting the monthly Eligible Mobile Telephone payment as soon as reasonably practicable for the month preceding the date the Eligible Mobile Telephone was subject to Theft or suffered Accidental Damage.  A copy of the Eligible Cardholder’s most recent mobile provider’s billing statement as soon as reasonably practicable showing the payment for the Monthly Mobile Plan.  If available, a copy of the original Eligible Mobile Telephone purchase receipt or other sufficient proof of the Eligible Mobile Telephone model currently linked to the Eligible Cardholder’s Eligible Mobile Telephone account as soon as reasonably practicable.  If the Eligible Cardholder has an existing insurance from the mobile operator, a copy of the insurance claim to the Eligible Cardholder’s mobile telephone insurance, or in the event that the claim amount is less than the Eligible Cardholder’s deductible, a copy of the policy’s declarations page as soon as reasonably practicable.  If a claim is due to Accidental Damage, a copy of the repair estimate and photos as soon as reasonably practicable of the Accidental Damage. We may also reasonably require You to send in the damaged Eligible Mobile Telephone(s), at Your expense, for further evaluation of Your claim.  If the claim is due to Theft, a copy of the police report filed as soon as reasonably practicable.  Frequently asked questions  If you need more information on mobile phone insurance, check out our frequently asked questions.  How to make a claim   Online   You can submit an online claim.  Submit your online claim , opens in new window   By clicking “Submit your online claim”, you will be leaving NAB’s website and going to a third party website (with its own terms of use and privacy policy). NAB is not responsible for the products, services and content on the third party website.  Email   For further assistance, you can contact AIG via email.  Send an email   Call   Call AIG Australia Limited Monday to Friday, 8:30am to 5:30pm (AEST/ADST).  Australia: +61 395 224 111  Explore other complimentary insurances   Your NAB card could be eligible for one or more of the following complimentary insurances.  Complimentary international travel insurance   If your card is eligible for complimentary international travel insurance, this is what you need to know.  Complimentary domestic travel insurance   Learn what you need to know about your domestic travel insurance.  Complimentary rental vehicle excess insurance in Australia   How to make a claim, check eligibility and understand your policy.  Important Information   Terms and Conditions  Disclaimer 1   Mobile phone insurance  Complimentary mobile phone Insurance (covering Accidental Damage and Theft to a mobile phone) is provided under a group policy issued by AIG Australia Limited (ABN 93 004 727 753, AFSL 381 686) (AIG) to National Australia Bank Limited (ABN 12 004 044 937, AFSL and Australian Credit Licence 230686) (NAB). Cover is available to all NAB Rewards Signature Credit cardholders (Eligible Person). Eligible Persons can claim under the Group Policy as third-party beneficiaries in accordance with section 48 of the Insurance Contracts Act 1984 (Cth).   If an Eligible Person wishes to claim, such claim will be subject to the eligibility criteria, terms, conditions, exclusions, limits and applicable sub-limits as further detailed in the terms and conditions (PDF, 145KB), opens in new window  for such coverage which is available at nab.com.au/personal/insurance/complimentary-card-insurances.     To be eligible for coverage Eligible Persons must have a contract with a mobile phone provider and a monthly billing arrangement using their eligible NAB card. Any advice provided by NAB about this insurance is general advice only and is not based on any consideration of an Eligible Person’s objectives, financial situation, or needs. Eligible Persons must determine whether or not it is appropriate, in light of their own circumstances, to act on this advice.   AIG as product issuer is not providing any financial advice about this insurance. NAB is not the product issuer, and neither NAB or its related bodies corporate guarantee any of the benefits under this insurance cover.    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.
